message:
fix: gnp_random_graph accepts p outside [0,1] matching nx (br-r37-c1-lsdek)

nx silently accepts any value of ``p`` in ``gnp_random_graph``::

    p >= 1   → complete graph (every possible edge present)
    p <= 0   → empty graph (no edges)

…the Python fallback code in ``gnp_random_graph`` already handles
both corner cases correctly.  But the Rust ``_rust_gnp_random_
graph`` binding strictly validates ``0.0 <= p <= 1.0`` and raises
a ``ValueError`` whose message leaks Rust internal types::

    ValueError: FailClosed { operation: "gnp_random_graph",
                             reason: "p=1.5 is outside [0.0, 1.0]" }

Both the class drift (ValueError vs nx's silent success) and the
Rust-internals leak in the message break drop-in parity.
``erdos_renyi_graph`` (alias) and ``binomial_graph`` (alias)
inherit the same defect via their delegation to gnp_random_graph.

Fix
---
``python/franken_networkx/__init__.py`` (``gnp_random_graph``):

- Gate the Rust fast-path on ``0.0 <= p <= 1.0``.  Values outside
  that range fall through to the existing Python fallback, which
  handles ``p >= 1`` (complete) and ``p <= 0`` (empty) explicitly.
  ``erdos_renyi_graph`` / ``binomial_graph`` get the fix transitively.

Tests
-----
New ``tests/python/test_gnp_random_graph_p_range_parity.py``:
- 3 × ``p > 1`` / ``p == 1`` / alias variants returning complete K_n
- 3 × ``p < 0`` / ``p == 0`` / alias variants returning empty graph
- 1 × binomial_graph alias parity
- 2 × in-range regression + negative-n NetworkXError invariant

Full sweep
----------
7126 passed, 0 failed (was 7117 before — +9 new regression cases).

Found via systematic argument-validation probe per the idle-nudge
self-review discipline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -111,6 +111,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-lim0x","title":"eulerian_circuit edge order/traversal direction drifts from nx","description":"fnx.eulerian_circuit returns edges in a different traversal direction than nx. nx walks the circuit in reverse vs. fnx's direction.

Repro:
  edges = [('a','b'),('b','c'),('c','a'),('a','d'),('d','e'),('e','a')]
  fnx -> [('a','b'),('b','c'),('c','a'),('a','d'),('d','e'),('e','a')]
  nx  -> [('a','e'),('e','d'),('d','a'),('a','c'),('c','b'),('b','a')]

  edges = [(0,1),(1,2),(2,3),(3,0)]
  fnx -> [(0,1),(1,2),(2,3),(3,0)]
  nx  -> [(0,3),(3,2),(2,1),(1,0)]

Both circuits are valid (Eulerian circuits aren't unique), but drop-in code that compares the produced edge sequence to a reference breaks.

Fix: delegate eulerian_circuit (and likely eulerian_path) to nx for parity.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:32:18.379695234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:35:17.333267853Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:35:17.331312850Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-lmviu","title":"parity: add_path/add_cycle/add_star keyword parameter names","description":"NetworkX exposes add_path(G_to_add_to, nodes_for_path, **attr), add_cycle(G_to_add_to, nodes_for_cycle, **attr), and add_star(G_to_add_to, nodes_for_star, **attr). FNX currently names these parameters G and nodes, so documented keyword calls like fnx.add_path(G_to_add_to=G, nodes_for_path=[1,2]) raise TypeError. Align the public signatures while preserving positional behavior and add parity tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-networkx","created_at":"2026-04-25T06:20:06.172080189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T06:23:19.168859030Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T06:23:19.167009410Z","close_reason":"Completed in 92bae80","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-lnqss","title":"generic_bfs_edges (Rust default-neighbors path) yields edges in wrong order","description":"fnx.generic_bfs_edges has two paths: a Python branch (when neighbors=callback is provided) that matches nx, and a Rust _fnx.generic_bfs_edges_rust branch (default neighbors=None) that yields edges in reversed adj order.

Repro:
  edges = [(c,d),(a,b),(b,c),(d,e),(a,c)]
  Both libs: adj[a] = [b, c]

  fnx.generic_bfs_edges(g, source=a) -> [(a,c),(a,b),(c,d),(d,e)]
  nx.generic_bfs_edges(gx, source=a) -> [(a,b),(a,c),(c,d),(d,e)]

  fnx.generic_bfs_edges(g, source=a, neighbors=g.neighbors) -> [(a,b),(a,c),...] (matches nx)

The plain bfs_edges variant matches nx; only generic_bfs_edges via the Rust default-neighbors path drifts.

Fix: route through the Python path always, defaulting neighbors to G.neighbors when None. The Python branch already matches nx exactly. Also remove the dead "if neighbors is None: neighbors = G.neighbors" line inside the "if neighbors is not None" block.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:01:23.195765293Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:03:53.615994642Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T19:03:53.613905654Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"br-r37-c1-lz7u6","title":"parity: connectivity bridge_components missing","description":"networkx.algorithms.connectivity.bridge_components(G) returns 2-edge-connected components after removing bridges. Not exposed by franken_networkx. Add Python wrapper delegating to nx.algorithms.connectivity.bridge_components via _networkx_graph_for_parity, export at top-level and add to __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T11:44:53.163889437Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:14:43.967902315Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:14:43.966070217Z","close_reason":"Already implemented in fnx — top-level export matches nx.connectivity exactly. Landed tests/python/test_connectivity_parity_extras.py (commit 3765516) with 37 cases freezing the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-m0io3","title":"fnx accepts unhashable types as nodes; nx raises TypeError","description":"fnx silently accepts unhashable types (list, set, dict) as graph nodes via add_node, add_edge, and add_edges_from. nx raises TypeError('unhashable type: ...').

Repro:
  >>> g = fnx.Graph()
  >>> g.add_node([1, 2])              # silently succeeds; nodes() = [[1, 2]]
  >>> nx.Graph().add_node([1, 2])
  TypeError: unhashable type: 'list'

  >>> g.add_edge([1, 2], 3)            # silently succeeds
  >>> nx.Graph().add_edge([1, 2], 3)
  TypeError: unhashable type: 'list'

  >>> g.add_edges_from([([1, 2], 3)])  # silently succeeds
  >>> nx.Graph().add_edges_from([([1, 2], 3)])
  TypeError: unhashable type: 'list'

Drop-in code that catches TypeError when constructing a graph from external/serialized data won't trigger on fnx — the bad input gets silently absorbed into the graph and breaks downstream operations later.

Hashable types continue to work on both libraries (tuple, frozenset, str, int, etc.). Only inherently-unhashable types (list, set, dict) drift.

Root cause: the Rust _raw_add_node binding accepts arbitrary Python objects without calling hash() for validation. The existing _make_none_rejecting_add_node wrapper guards against None but not unhashable types.

Fix: extend the add_node wrapper to call hash(node) (which raises TypeError for unhashable types matching nx's exact behavior) before delegating to the Rust impl. Apply to add_edge / add_edges_from as well.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T09:17:16.206399832Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T09:23:12.717050809Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T09:23:12.714273551Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-m4msi","title":"parity: utils.random_sequence powerlaw_sequence + zipf_rv + cumulative_distribution + is_valid_tree_degree_sequence (4 functions)","description":"Four random-sequence utilities from networkx.utils.random_sequence not surfaced by franken_networkx: powerlaw_sequence(n, exponent=2.0, seed=None), zipf_rv(alpha, xmin=1, seed=None), cumulative_distribution(distribution), is_valid_tree_degree_sequence(degree_sequence). These are used by stochastic graph generators and degree-sequence sanity checks. Add Python wrappers in __init__.py exporting at top-level and adding to __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T21:59:39.857059179Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:18:46.320103675Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:18:46.318235318Z","close_reason":"Already implemented. powerlaw_sequence/zipf_rv/cumulative_distribution exposed at fnx top level matching nx.utils bit-for-bit when seeded; discrete_sequence on fnx.utils; is_valid_tree_degree_sequence is fnx-only ((bool, reason) tuple). Landed tests/python/test_random_sequence_parity.py (commit d2ed994) with 89 cases freezing the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -14505,7 +14505,14 @@ def gnp_random_graph(n, p, seed=None, directed=False, *, create_using=None, back
     # n crashes with a Python type-conversion error).
     if n < 0:
         raise NetworkXError(f"Negative number of nodes not valid: {n}")
-    if not directed and create_using is None and (seed is None or isinstance(seed, int)):
+    # br-r37-c1-lsdek: nx silently accepts ``p`` outside [0, 1] —
+    # ``p >= 1`` produces the complete graph, ``p <= 0`` produces
+    # the empty graph (the Python fallback below handles both
+    # cases).  fnx's Rust ``_rust_gnp_random_graph`` validates p
+    # strictly and raises ``ValueError("FailClosed { ... p=... is
+    # outside [0.0, 1.0] }")`` (Rust internals leak too).  Route
+    # out-of-range p through the Python path for parity.
+    if not directed and create_using is None and 0.0 <= p <= 1.0 and (seed is None or isinstance(seed, int)):
         return _rust_gnp_random_graph(n, p, seed=_native_random_seed(seed))
 
     rng = _generator_random_state(seed)

@@ -0,0 +1,121 @@
+"""Parity for gnp_random_graph (and erdos_renyi_graph alias) with
+``p`` outside [0, 1].
+
+Bead br-r37-c1-lsdek.
+
+nx silently accepts any ``p``::
+
+    p >= 1   → complete graph (every possible edge present)
+    p <= 0   → empty graph (no edges)
+
+The Rust ``_rust_gnp_random_graph`` binding validates p strictly
+and raises a ``ValueError`` whose message leaks Rust internals::
+
+    ValueError: FailClosed { operation: "gnp_random_graph",
+                             reason: "p=1.5 is outside [0.0, 1.0]" }
+
+Both the class and the message format break drop-in code.
+
+Fix: gate the Rust path on ``0.0 <= p <= 1.0`` so out-of-range
+values fall through to the existing Python fallback (which already
+handles the p>=1 / p<=0 corner cases correctly).
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import pytest
+
+import franken_networkx as fnx
+
+try:
+    import networkx as nx
+    HAS_NX = True
+except ImportError:
+    HAS_NX = False
+
+needs_nx = pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NX, reason="networkx not installed")
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# p > 1 → complete graph
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_gnp_random_graph_p_above_one_returns_complete():
+    f = fnx.gnp_random_graph(5, 1.5, seed=42)
+    n = nx.gnp_random_graph(5, 1.5, seed=42)
+    assert sorted(f.edges()) == sorted(n.edges())
+    # Sanity: K_5 has C(5, 2) = 10 edges
+    assert f.number_of_edges() == 10
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_erdos_renyi_alias_p_above_one_returns_complete():
+    f = fnx.erdos_renyi_graph(5, 1.5, seed=42)
+    n = nx.erdos_renyi_graph(5, 1.5, seed=42)
+    assert sorted(f.edges()) == sorted(n.edges())
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_gnp_random_graph_p_exactly_one_returns_complete():
+    f = fnx.gnp_random_graph(5, 1, seed=42)
+    n = nx.gnp_random_graph(5, 1, seed=42)
+    assert sorted(f.edges()) == sorted(n.edges())
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# p < 0 → empty graph
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_gnp_random_graph_p_below_zero_returns_empty():
+    f = fnx.gnp_random_graph(5, -0.5, seed=42)
+    n = nx.gnp_random_graph(5, -0.5, seed=42)
+    assert list(f.edges()) == []
+    assert list(n.edges()) == []
+    assert f.number_of_nodes() == n.number_of_nodes() == 5
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_erdos_renyi_alias_p_below_zero_returns_empty():
+    f = fnx.erdos_renyi_graph(5, -1, seed=42)
+    n = nx.erdos_renyi_graph(5, -1, seed=42)
+    assert list(f.edges()) == list(n.edges()) == []
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_gnp_random_graph_p_exactly_zero_returns_empty():
+    f = fnx.gnp_random_graph(5, 0, seed=42)
+    n = nx.gnp_random_graph(5, 0, seed=42)
+    assert list(f.edges()) == list(n.edges()) == []
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Binomial alias and dense_gnm sanity
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_binomial_graph_alias_p_above_one():
+    f = fnx.binomial_graph(5, 2, seed=42)
+    n = nx.binomial_graph(5, 2, seed=42)
+    assert sorted(f.edges()) == sorted(n.edges())
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Regressions — in-range p still goes through Rust path
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_gnp_random_graph_in_range_p_unchanged():
+    f = fnx.gnp_random_graph(10, 0.3, seed=42)
+    n = nx.gnp_random_graph(10, 0.3, seed=42)
+    assert f.number_of_nodes() == n.number_of_nodes() == 10
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_gnp_random_graph_negative_n_still_raises_networkx_error():
+    """Pre-existing br-gnpvalid invariant: n<0 raises NetworkXError."""
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXError, match=r"Negative number of nodes not valid"):
+        fnx.gnp_random_graph(-1, 0.5, seed=42)
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NetworkXError, match=r"Negative number of nodes not valid"):
+        nx.gnp_random_graph(-1, 0.5, seed=42)
